Implemented scripts/reset_password.py + tests, all passing

Implemented in dd2de26: main.py gates create_all behind DTASKS_AUTO_CREATE_SCHEMA (tests opt in via conftest.py), alembic is authoritative for prod. Verified alembic upgrade head on a fresh DB produces an equivalent schema to create_all, and full test suite (30 tests) passes.

Dockerfile (multi-stage: node frontend build + python/uv runtime), docker-compose.yml (app + Caddy, named volume for DB+sessions), Caddyfile (auto-HTTPS via {$DOMAIN}/{$ACME_EMAIL}), docker-entrypoint.sh (alembic upgrade head then uvicorn), .dockerignore, and .env.example added. Fixed a real bug found during review: DTASKS_DB_PATH was set to a subdirectory (/data/db/...) that SQLite can't auto-create; changed to /data/dtasks.sqlite directly under the volume root. End-to-end smoke test on this machine (after fixing a local broken Docker/OrbStack install) confirmed: anonymous /api/* returns 401; signup/login set-cookie has Secure+HttpOnly+SameSite=lax; alembic migrations run automatically at container startup; docker compose down && up (without -v) preserves the user account, confirming DB/session persistence via the named volume.

Wrote docs/deploy.md covering prerequisites, first deploy, env var reference, migrations, password reset, backup/restore, invite codes, and the multi-app-on-one-VM note. Every command in it was run and verified against the live stack: full backup->drift->restore drill (created a post-backup user, restored, confirmed it's gone and the pre-backup user survives) and reset_password.py run inside a live container (old password fails after reset, new one works). Also fixed two real gaps found while verifying: scripts/ wasn't copied into the image (reset_password.py couldn't run), and sqlite3 CLI wasn't installed in the slim python image (the documented .backup command would have failed) -- both added to the Dockerfile. README's Production section now links to the runbook.
Merged in PR #40. Verified: terraform apply ran clean against eu-frankfurt-1, VM reachable via SSH with cloud-init done, Docker Engine running, iptables 80/443 open.

Implemented in PR https://github.com/evgenii-prusov/dtasks/pull/56 (branch oauth-schema-foundation-dtask-5bv-1). Full test suite passes (32/32); migration verified end-to-end (upgrade head / downgrade -1) on a seeded SQLite DB.
Fixed in PR #57: ci.yml push trigger scoped to main (stops duplicate CI runs on PR branches); main branch protection now requires the 3 CI job status checks before merge.

Verified live on dtasks.dev: HTTPS active, 1 real user account exists, session cookie has Secure+HttpOnly+SameSite=lax, unauthenticated /api/projects returns 401, alembic at head (confirmed via SSH during migration verification).
All 4 children closed and dtask-jzw (first deploy) verified live and complete.
All 4 children complete; acceptance criteria verified live on dtasks.dev (HTTPS, invite-gated signup, per-user isolation, unauthenticated /api/* -> 401).
PR https://github.com/evgenii-prusov/dtasks/pull/59 open, CI green (Backend lint+test, Frontend lint+type-check+test, Terraform fmt+validate all pass), full backend suite 53 passed.

PR https://github.com/evgenii-prusov/dtasks/pull/60 open, CI green (Backend lint+test, Frontend lint+type-check+test, Terraform fmt+validate all pass). docs/auth.md checked line-by-line against merged backend (backend/app/oauth.py, backend/app/auth.py) -- no drift found, doc already matches shipped code.
PR https://github.com/evgenii-prusov/dtasks/pull/61 open, all 3 required CI checks (Backend, Frontend, Terraform) green.

Verified live on dtasks.dev for both providers. Google: redirect_uri correct after fixing Cloud Console config, real token exchange succeeded (httpx logs show 200 OK, no token values logged), auto-linked to existing password account by verified email (no duplicate user). GitHub: same -- redirect_uri correct, real token exchange succeeded, auto-linked to the same user (oauth_accounts now has 2 rows, users still has 1). Remaining checklist items (fresh signup+invite gating, OAuth-only 401, provider-deny banner) are covered by the automated suites that gated merge: 21 respx-mocked backend tests (test_oauth.py) and WelcomeView frontend tests exercise those exact branches; not re-verified manually since they'd need a second test identity.

All 5 children complete. Google and GitHub login verified end-to-end in production (dtasks.dev): correct redirect URIs, real token exchange, auto-link by verified email with no duplicate accounts, no provider tokens persisted or logged.
